records.summary: Emulsion polymerization recipes usually depend on a small set of roles: monomers/comonomers, water or aqueous phase, surfactant package, initiator system, buffer or pH control, optional chain-transfer agent, and optional crosslinker.
guidance: Record reagent identity and physical properties in Master Reagents, then reference reagent_id from Formulations.
Keep monomer composition, surfactant level, initiator level, and feed profile explicit because they drive most interpretation.
Do not treat a recommendation as executable until SDS, SOP, and operating limits are reviewed.

records.summary: Particle size and colloidal stability are commonly affected by surfactant type/level, seed design, ionic strength, solids, agitation, and monomer feed rate.
guidance: When particle size is high, avoid changing monomer chemistry first; isolate surfactant package or feed duration.
When coagulum appears, capture where it formed, when it appeared, and whether it tracks feed, pH, temperature, or agitation.
Use DLS method details and sample dilution notes so changes are not instrument artifacts.
search_terms: emulsion polymerization surfactant particle size coagulum latex stability feed rate

records.summary: Initiator concentration, thermal profile, oxygen removal, and chase feed strategy influence conversion and residual monomer.
guidance: If conversion is low, verify initiator freshness, purge quality, and temperature before changing recipe composition.
Separate nucleation-stage initiator effects from chase-stage conversion effects in the Formulations tab.
Use the same conversion method across follow-up experiments.
search_terms: emulsion polymerization persulfate initiator conversion residual monomer oxygen inhibition

records.summary: Core-shell latex experiments need explicit core/shell ratio, core Tg target, shell composition, crosslinker level, and particle size target.
guidance: Do not interpret toughening performance without recording particle size, shell composition, and matrix compatibility.
Use staged formulation rows to distinguish core feed, shell feed, and chase additions.
search_terms: core shell latex acrylic impact modifier particle size Tg shell composition emulsion polymerization

records.summary: Legacy reaction sheets often store one experiment per column, with rows grouped into sections such as Inputs, Outputs, Calculations, Reaction Parameters, Results, Notes, and Observations.
guidance: When ingesting a reaction sheet, detect whether experiment IDs live across columns rather than down rows.
Section labels such as Inputs, Outputs, Calculations, Reaction Parameters, Results, Notes, and Observations should become structured fields before recommendation.
Preserve both planned values and weighed or observed values because old sheets often keep calculated masses next to actual net masses.
Treat notes and visual observations as result context, not just comments, because they often explain mixing, viscosity, cure, workup, or transfer failures.
source_evidence: Google Drive sampled sheets: Baker's Reaction Sheet.xlsx, 2026 02 Sperry Rails Reactions, Pyrrolidone Monomer Synthesis, Nylon 4 Synthesis, Castor Oil Thiolation and Aminated Epoxidized Soybean Oil Synthesis, CTA Synthesis.
search_terms: reaction sheet columnar experiments inputs outputs calculations reaction parameters notes observations legacy spreadsheet

records.summary: RAFT polymer reaction sheets commonly calculate target molecular weight from monomer, chain-transfer agent, initiator ratio, conversion target, solvent ratio, inhibitor concentration, and reaction temperature.
guidance: Record target molecular weight, desired conversion, monomer amount, solvent ratio, and initiator-to-CTA ratio before interpreting a RAFT polymerization result.
Keep CTA identity separate from initiator identity because the CTA controls target DP while initiator controls radical flux.
For viscous or bio-based monomers, capture inhibitor concentration, functionality, solvent mass, and actual CTA/initiator solution masses because these often explain drift from the target.
Follow-up suggestions should change one axis at a time: CTA ratio, initiator ratio, solvent fraction, temperature, or inhibitor handling.
source_evidence: Baker's Reaction Sheet.xlsx tabs RAFT Polymer (BA), RAFT Polymer (AESO), CyCART/OXCART-related sheets.
search_terms: RAFT polymerization target molecular weight AIBN CTA trithiocarbonate solvent ratio conversion inhibitor reaction temperature

records.summary: Polyurethane and elastomer reaction sheets commonly track NCO index, total OH basis, OH fractions, polyol/crosslinker masses, catalyst master batch, net weighed masses, hardness, texture, working time, and mixing limits.
guidance: Validate OH fraction sums and calculated NCO index before recommending a polyurethane follow-up.
Capture gross calculated masses and net weighed masses separately so mix or transfer errors can be diagnosed.
Use hardness, working time, viscosity rise, bubbles, crumbly texture, and transfer difficulty as result signals.
If a sample cures too fast or becomes mass-transfer limited, adjust chain extender ratio, catalyst level, prepolymer order, or mixing sequence before changing the entire formulation.
source_evidence: 2026 02 Sperry Rails Reactions tabs Experimental log and Results.
search_terms: polyurethane NCO index OH fraction catalyst master batch hardness Shore A working time viscosity mixing crumbly

records.summary: Pyrrolidone and Nylon 4 synthesis sheets track target product mass, lysine or pyrrolidone concentration, solvent/cosolvent choice, temperature, RPM, duration, distillation or CO2 addition conditions, and NMR-derived formation metrics.
guidance: For pyrrolidone formation, record precursor concentration, solvent choice, temperature, RPM, duration, and whether acid hydrolysis or distillation was used.
For Nylon 4 polymerization, keep predry and postdry masses, base equivalents, phase-transfer salt concentration, CO2 pressure/flow, and addition order explicit.
NMR integral ratios and formation percent should be normalized into Results so follow-up suggestions can connect conditions to conversion.
When a note mentions distillation, vacuum, CO2 addition, or hydrolysis, treat it as a process-stage variable for follow-up planning.
source_evidence: Pyrrolidone Monomer Synthesis tab PA4-M-8 and Nylon 4 Synthesis tab PA4-10.
search_terms: Nylon 4 pyrrolidone synthesis lysine acetophenone TBuOK TBAHS CO2 NMR formation percent distillation hydrolysis

records.summary: Bio-based oil derivatization sheets track substrate ratios, catalyst or inhibitor identity, solvent loading, stepwise feed order, water removal, and molar balance for thiolation, amination, amidation, and isocyanate reactions.
guidance: Capture catalyst, inhibitor, and solvent identity alongside mass charges because these rows are often separated from the main material list.
For Dean-Stark or water-producing reactions, record theoretical and actual water collected as a conversion or completion signal.
For amination or isocyanate reactions, preserve feed order and hold time because the sheets distinguish heat-up, syringe feed, and overnight hold steps.
Follow-up suggestions should reference molar balance, water removal, catalyst loading, and observed notes before changing substrate chemistry.
source_evidence: Castor Oil Thiolation and Aminated Epoxidized Soybean Oil Synthesis tabs Master Sheet, Amination-11, and Thiolation-2.
search_terms: bio based oil thiolation amination epoxidized soybean oil MAA HESO PTSA Dean Stark water production IPDI DBTDL

records.summary: RAFT CTA synthesis sheets commonly record thiol, carbon disulfide, base, water, ketone or sulfonyl chloride electrophile, solvent, addition order, temperature control, and reaction timing.
guidance: CTA synthesis planning should preserve stoichiometric equivalents, addition sequence, and temperature-control notes, not just final reagent totals.
Dropwise additions, ice bath, reflux, and timed holds are process-stage observations that should become Daily Log entries.
If a CTA is later used in polymerization, link the CTA synthesis evidence to the Master Reagents lot or notes field.
source_evidence: CTA Synthesis tabs CyCART-1 and OXCART-1.
search_terms: RAFT CTA synthesis trithiocarbonate carbon disulfide thiol potassium hydroxide addition order ice bath OXCART CyCART

records.summary: A useful lab notebook agent needs structured reagent identities, exact quantities, timestamps, observations, measurements, and links to evidence before it can recommend a defensible next run.
guidance: Make experiment_id and reagent_id stable because they are join keys across tabs.
Free-text observations are valuable, but measurements should be normalized into Results where possible.
Literature Evidence should cite DOI or URL and separate findings from interpretation.
search_terms: electronic lab notebook structured data experiment recommendation literature evidence
